description: first native-CUDA port: fp32 LayerNorm, one CTA/row, load row once + two block reductions (mean, then sum((x-mean)^2)/N), affine; matches Triton numerics

evidence.correctness: KDA_RUN_CORRECTNESS=1 KDA_REQUIRE_CUDA=1 pytest -> 39 passed; helios [8640,5120] fp32 max_abs_diff 9.5e-7; adversarial vs fp64 ref

notes: correctness-first, not yet optimized (vals[32] register pressure; scalar loads). Baseline numbers + optimization next.

description: first native-CUDA port: bf16 RMSNorm D=128, one warp/row (32 lanes x 4 bf16 via 64-bit vec load), fp32 square accum + warp-shuffle reduce, scale by w, bf16 store

evidence.correctness: all 5 production RMS shapes incl [650040,128] pass; smoke [64,128] max_abs_diff 0.0

notes: correctness-first; per-S config tuning + bandwidth/occupancy optimization (esp. S=1320 small-launch, S~650K bandwidth) next.

description: naive first-port baseline vs SGLang on the 6 production shapes (B200 GPU 3, idle before/after)

description: optimized fp32 LayerNorm: float4 vectorized loads/stores, vals[<=5] float4 (<=20 regs vs 32), parallel warp-shuffle block reduction; keeps sum((x-mean)^2)/N + fp32 division. Lifts helios 0.59x -> 1.19x.

evidence.correctness: 56/56 correctness (incl 15 fallback) + 48/48 fused oracle on B200

sources: docs/optimization_directions.md (Family A top pick: exact-N float4 single-CTA/row)
notes: RMS kernels unchanged this round. 5/6 production shapes parity-or-better (helios 1.19x; RMS 1320/4096/16384 1.7-1.8x). Remaining regressions: large RMS 648720/650040 ~0.90x -> task10 (round 4). Supersedes cand-0001-bench for LN.

description: large-S RMS MLP attempt: kUnroll-templated grid-stride, w loaded once/warp. kUnroll=4 best (~77us) but still ~0.84-0.92x vs Triton baseline for 648720/650040.

evidence.interleaved_ab: kUnroll 1/2/4/8 vs baseline @S=650040: best kUnroll=4 ratio~0.92 (min); kUnroll=1~0.68-0.99

notes: NO-GO: warp-per-row cannot beat the Triton 16-row tile for huge bandwidth-streaming RMS. Active bound: memory-latency + SM-issue.

description: final dispatch: fp32 LN float4 (cand-0002) + bf16 RMS one-warp/row for small/mid (CUDA) + large-S (648720,650040) FALLBACK to Triton baseline (no-go). Benchmark interleaved (A/B per-iter) to cancel GPU clock drift.

notes: AC-D/AC-F final lineage. Confirms cand-0004-dispatch within GPU clock drift (kernel-event geomean 1.328x ~ 1.327x); all 6 production shapes parity-or-better (4 CUDA promotions + 2 large-S baseline-fallback parity). benchmark.csv notes now carry baseline + candidate median/mean/std/min/p10/p90. source_hash is repo-relative (supersedes cand-0004's basename-keyed 26198958). [Post-benchmark review-phase fixes (rounds 7-8) modify register.py ROUTING logic only -- hintless _infer_hint disambiguation and the data_ptr alignment gate -- which the benchmark does not exercise (it routes via explicit dispatcher_hint with aligned, freshly-allocated tensors). The measured kernel numbers are therefore unchanged from source_hash 5664640bc35ca28b and were not re-run; only the .cuh + kernel launch determine measured performance.]
